title: ChargePoint Holdings (CHPT) Stock Forecast & Analysis
text: ChargePoint Holdings, Inc. provides electric vehicle EV charging technology solutions and offers a range of networked charging systems, charger management systems, and e-mobility service provider solutions. In fiscal year 2026, ChargePoint Holdings's revenue was $411.22 million, a decrease of negative 1.40% compared to the previous year's $417.08 million. During its 2024 fiscal year, its revenue from networked charging stations dropped by around 1%, and its net loss ballooned from $345 million in fiscal 2023 to $457 million. According to 9 analysts, the average rating for CHPT stock is Hold, with a 12-month stock price target of $9.25, which is an increase of 35.83% from the latest price. However, since peaking in late 2020, ChargePoint stock has fallen by 95%. ChargePoint operates a total of 56,000 ports across 31,000 locations, giving it the largest EV public charging network in the U.S., ahead of Tesla and Blink Charging, and when you include its European assets, ChargePoint has over 286,000 ports. The stock has plummeted by 65% this year due to delayed compatibility with Tesla's charging network, significant stock dilution and distant profitability prospects. Weaker consumer demand has also weighed on the EV industry's growth. Between 2024 and 2032, the EV charging market is projected to expand at a CAGR of 27%.
